title: Oreoseris tanantii

It is native to China and is often classified within the genus Oreoseris, which consists of a small group of flowering plants. Oreoseris tanantii is a type of annual or biennial plant that produces small, white or yellowish flowers. The classification of Oreoseris tanantii at the subfamily or tribe level is within the subfamily Cichorioideae or the tribe Cichorieae. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Oreoseris tanantii, also known as the Indian pennywort, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Asteraceae. The Indian pennywort has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic effects.
